svg.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif
svg.title: Queen MQ vs RabbitMQ: 10k ordered streams without 10k Erlang processes
svg.desc: To get per-entity ordering in RabbitMQ you allocate one queue per entity, which means one Erlang process, file handle, and memory footprint per entity. With 10k entities that is 10k processes and roughly 2.4 GB of RAM. Queen gets the same per-entity ordering with one queue containing 10k logical partitions, costing 52 MB total and zero extra processes.

g.text: one queue • logical partitions per entity
to give every chat / user / tenant its own FIFO lane, you allocate one queue per entity
each = 1 erlang process • 1 file handle • ~244 KB heap
scheduler load • per-process state • per-process gc
one per persistent queue, hits ulimit fast
every entity gets its own FIFO partition inside a single queue. partitions = index rows.
+ 9,988 more partitions • just index rows in PostgreSQL
one C++ binary regardless of partition count
db pool + acceptors, fixed regardless of cardinality
file-handle & scheduler ceilings on a single node
